tasks.title: Shared diagnostic model and redaction core
tasks.definition_of_done: opi-agent exposes a shared Diagnostic model with severity, stable snake_case code, source, message, optional redacted structured details, and optional action fields; serialization is deterministic, severity ordering is tested, known secrets and sensitive content are redacted by default, and human formatting stays out of lower runtime layers.

inference_notes.reason: Draft DoD inferred from the registered Phase 7 design sections for the Diagnostic Model and redaction requirements; the user accepted the reviewed six-task Phase 7 graph during initialization.

Phase 7 must not implement remote telemetry, analytics collection, automatic session sharing, package ecosystem expansion, OAuth/provider breadth work, a new tracing backend unless existing tracing cannot satisfy requirements, full prompt or tool output capture by default, a web dashboard, or a stable 1.0 observability protocol.
Phase 7 diagnostic primitives build on the completed Phase 6 alignment hardening task 6.6.
Shared model and redaction helpers belong in opi-agent first, with app-specific extensions layered above.
The task adds new diagnostic runtime API rather than only tests or docs.
Diagnostic types and tests live under opi-agent; no app or provider call sites are required until later Phase 7 rows.

evidence.verification: library; cargo test -p opi-agent --test diagnostics (16 passed); cargo fmt --check --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc --workspace --no-deps clean

session_notes.summary: Implemented shared Diagnostic model + redaction core via TDD (red->green). 16 diagnostics tests green; fmt/clippy -D warnings/doc -D warnings clean. Independent 4-lens evaluator workflow: all pass, no blockers. Non-blocking notes: (1) SecretRedactor field list omits authorization/bearer header names - pre-existing in task 4.10, outside 7.1 DoD; (2) args/tool_result coverage now pinned; (3) ABSOLUTE_PATH_RE POSIX branch over-redacts URLs containing dir names (e.g. https://Users/foo) - safety-biased, never leaks, https://example.com confirmed not matched. Substrate-only: no production call site wired (task 7.2+).

gate_results.smoke: passed at Phase A.3 (pre-change workspace health baseline)

tasks.title: Provider/runtime diagnostic classification
tasks.definition_of_done: Provider errors, retry/backoff, cancellation, compaction, tool failures, package adapter degradation, session storage/recovery, config parse/model resolution, and RPC startup problems emit or expose shared Diagnostic values with stable source/code/severity/action, redacted details, and unchanged runtime behavior; fixture and integration tests cover provider classification without network plus retry, cancellation, compaction, adapter degradation, provider failure, and corrupt-line recovery diagnostics.

evidence.opi_acceptance: phase7-runtime-failure-diagnostics (partial); agent_loop paths closed; harness emission deferred to 7.3/7.5

session_notes.summary: Phase B confirmed (boundary-respecting scope). 7.2 owns: CODE_* constants + From<&E> classification for all error families; minimal DiagnosticSink/RecordingSink for test-observable emission; wire provider/retry/cancel/tool/compaction/session-recovery/package-adapter/config/rpc failure paths to emit (no behavior change). DEFER to 7.3: durable trace envelope; 7.4: opi doctor; 7.5: rpc_ready wire-shape (startup_diagnostics Vec<String>->Vec<Diagnostic>) migration + serialization. Spec drift check passed (both spec files hash-aligned). Smoke 64 tests green.

Phase E: marked passing. Scope = classification substrate across all error families + production emission through real agent_loop (retry/cancel/provider/tool) via DiagnosticSink + package/config bridges. Harness-level emission for session-recovery/compaction/package-adapter/config/RPC deferred to 7.3 (durable trace sink) + 7.5 (RPC exposure) per boundary-respecting scope confirmed by user. Evaluator: PASS-WITH-NITS (3 non-blocking nits: hook-deny classified as tool_execution_failed, retry_succeeded Info, double-emit on exhaustion — all noted for 7.3). Acceptance scenario phase7-runtime-failure-diagnostics left OPEN: agent_loop paths (retry/cancel/provider/tool) closed through production emission; compaction/session-recovery/adapter/config classification-ready, harness emission owed by 7.3/7.5.

tasks.title: Local trace envelope and trace sink
tasks.definition_of_done: opi-agent provides an unstable versioned local trace envelope with monotonic sequence numbers, run/turn/provider/tool/diagnostic-linked record structure, summary and verbose redaction modes, and trace sink failure handling that fails closed before a run and emits a diagnostic then disables the sink during a run; no trace is persisted automatically or forever.

inference_notes.reason: Draft DoD inferred from the registered Phase 7 design sections for the Trace Model and trace sink requirements; the user accepted the reviewed six-task Phase 7 graph during initialization.

Phase 7 must not implement remote telemetry, analytics collection, automatic session sharing, package ecosystem expansion, OAuth/provider breadth work, a new tracing backend unless existing tracing cannot satisfy requirements, full prompt or tool output capture by default, a web dashboard, or a stable 1.0 observability protocol.
Trace records attach to diagnostics and runtime classification introduced by 7.2.
The envelope and sink are opi-agent runtime primitives before CLI/RPC exposure.
The task adds new trace runtime API.
Trace primitives and tests are housed in opi-agent; user-facing request paths are owned by 7.5.

evidence.opi_verification: library; cargo test -p opi-agent --test trace_envelope (21 passed); fmt clean; clippy -D warnings clean; doc -D warnings clean; smoke green

session_notes.summary: Phase B user gate confirmed ('go on'). Proceeding to TDD (Phase C): new opi-agent trace module = unstable versioned envelope + monotonic sequence + run/turn/provider/tool/diagnostic-linked records + Summary/Verbose redaction + fail-closed prepare / fail-open write. agent_loop wiring deferred to 7.5 (substrate_only=true). Working tree clean at start; baseline dirty files empty.

Phase C/D/E complete. TDD red (compile-fail: trace module absent) -> green (21 tests). Independent 5-lens adversarial evaluator: verdict PASS, 0 confirmed high/critical, every DoD clause met. Addressed medium/low notes before commit: FileTraceSink switched BufWriter->LineWriter (flushes per newline = crash-resilient post-mortem trace); added single-threaded-emission doc note; added 5 tests (emit-after-finish no-op, finish()-error swallowed+disable, nested-recursive redaction, fail-open with diagnostics=None, write-before-prepare guard) and strengthened the two redaction tests with an absolute-path field to pin the Summary-vs-Verbose boundary symmetrically. Boundary respected: agent_loop wiring deferred to 7.5 (substrate_only=true, no acceptance_scenarios). Gates: fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, doc -D warnings clean, full opi-agent suite green (no regression), smoke passed.

gate_results.evaluator: PASS (5 lenses, adversarial verify, 0 confirmed high/critical)

tasks.title: Top-level opi doctor command
tasks.definition_of_done: The opi binary exposes top-level opi doctor, opi doctor --json, and opi doctor --scope config,provider,package,session,tui,rpc; it runs no paid model calls or network checks by default, keeps opi package doctor distinct, reports shared diagnostics for every requested scope, and follows exit code policy 0 for no errors or warnings only, 2 for one or more diagnostic errors, and 1 for internal doctor command failure.

inference_notes.reason: Draft DoD inferred from the registered Phase 7 design sections for the top-level opi doctor surface; the user accepted the reviewed six-task Phase 7 graph during initialization.

Phase 7 must not implement remote telemetry, analytics collection, automatic session sharing, package ecosystem expansion, OAuth/provider breadth work, a new tracing backend unless existing tracing cannot satisfy requirements, full prompt or tool output capture by default, a web dashboard, or a stable 1.0 observability protocol.
Doctor consumes the diagnostic model and trace primitives established by 7.1 through 7.3.
The task is a CLI/runtime feature in opi-coding-agent with terminal capability input from opi-tui.
The task adds a new user-facing CLI command.
Doctor command, subprocess tests, and terminal capability helpers live under opi-coding-agent and opi-tui.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: Running opi doctor --json --scope config,provider,package,session,tui,rpc reports config, selected provider metadata/credential presence, package and adapter diagnostics, session storage, TUI terminal capability, and RPC schema/startup diagnostics without network calls.
production_call_sites: opi_coding_agent::config layered config and model resolution
opi_coding_agent::config layered config and model resolution

evidence.opi_verification: cli-runtime; cargo test -p opi-coding-agent --test doctor_cli (28 passed); fmt clean; clippy -D warnings clean; doc -D warnings clean; smoke green
evidence.opi_acceptance: phase7-doctor-all-scopes (closed); all six production call sites exercised end-to-end via `opi doctor --json --scope ...`; 28 tests (scopes, NDJSON shape, exit policy 0/2/1, network-free, credential-value non-leak, package-doctor distinctness, details redaction)

session_notes.summary: Phase B user gate confirmed ('go on'). Proceeding to TDD (Phase C): new opi-coding-agent doctor module = top-level `opi doctor` / `--json` / `--scope config,provider,package,session,tui,rpc`, shared-Diagnostic scope checks, network-free by default, exit policy 0(warn/info)/2(error)/1(internal). Distinct from `opi package doctor` (unchanged). Wiring in main.rs before provider construction. Working tree clean at start; baseline dirty files empty.

Phase C/D/E complete. TDD red (compile-fail: doctor module absent) -> green (28 tests). New opi-coding-agent doctor module: DoctorScope (6 scopes, parse_list, ALL), DoctorContext (pure, env-var probe for credential PRESENCE only), run_doctor, format_text/NDJSON, exit policy 0(warn/info)/2(error)/1(internal scope-parse failure). cli.rs Doctor variant; main.rs run_doctor_cli dispatched before provider construction (network-free). Distinct from `opi package doctor` (unchanged). 5-lens adversarial evaluator: all 10 DoD clauses met; initial verdict NEEDS-WORK on one actionable cluster (format_json emitted Diagnostic.details RAW, leaking absolute config paths via diagnostic_from_config details.path, test-exercised). FIXED in-scope: route details through redacted_details(Summary) at the --json boundary (doctor.rs, 7.4-owned); pinned by doctor_json_redacts_absolute_path_in_config_details. Residuals: (a) credentialed git URL in package details.package_source needs SecretRedactor ghp_/userinfo patterns in opi-agent -> 7.6 redaction-guard scope (outside 7.4 owned paths); (b) provider scope reports model identity not capability metadata (network-free by design; DoD only requires shared diagnostics per scope); (c) spec example line 142 lists 5 scopes (doc nit, 7.4 owns no docs). Tightened package_doctor test to pin exit 0. Gates: fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, doc -D warnings clean, full opi-coding-agent suite green, smoke passed.

gate_results.evaluator: all DoD clauses met; actionable redaction finding fixed + pinned; residual URL gap deferred to 7.6

tasks.title: JSON/RPC diagnostics and trace exposure
tasks.definition_of_done: JSON mode and RPC expose diagnostics consistently: startup diagnostics appear before accepted prompt output, run summaries include structured diagnostic counts, trace events are versioned and distinguishable from normal agent events, unsupported trace requests fail with structured responses, non-interactive diagnostics remain backward-compatible, and trace request paths produce redacted trace envelopes without silently changing existing JSON/RPC semantics.

inference_notes.reason: Phase 7 must not implement remote telemetry, analytics collection, automatic session sharing, package ecosystem expansion, OAuth/provider breadth work, a new tracing backend unless existing tracing cannot satisfy requirements, full prompt or tool output capture by default, a web dashboard, or a stable 1.0 observability protocol.
JSON/RPC exposure depends on doctor and the prior diagnostic/trace primitives.
The task changes workspace-level event framing across opi-agent and opi-coding-agent.
The task adds additive protocol/event behavior.
JSON/RPC and non-interactive surfaces live in opi-coding-agent and consume opi-agent trace/session event primitives.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: JSON mode and RPC startup/run flows expose startup diagnostics and structured run-summary diagnostic counts before accepted prompt output while preserving existing additive 0.x event semantics.

When a trace is requested for a run through a supported CLI/config/RPC path, the run emits a versioned redacted trace envelope with run, turn, provider, tool, compaction, and diagnostic-linked records; unsupported trace requests return structured errors.

Retry, cancellation, compaction, adapter degradation, and provider failure paths represented by contract-stable Phase 7 events create diagnostics and trace records; runtime events that are not contract-stable remain diagnostic-linked rather than permanent trace kinds.

touched_files: tests: trace_envelope(+7 wiring), json_mode(+3 phase7), rpc_jsonl(+3 phase7), non_interactive + 6 opi-agent ctx sites

evidence.opi_verification: workspace; trace_envelope 28 passed (+7 phase7 wiring); json_mode phase7 3; rpc_jsonl phase7 3; fmt/clippy(-D warnings)/doc(-D warnings) clean; full workspace test 0 failures; smoke green
evidence.opi_evaluator: passed (5-lens adversarial; 2 confirmed findings fixed + pinned: per-run RPC diagnostic counts via RecordingSink::clear, run_summary ordered after AgentEnd)
evidence.opi_acceptance: phase7-json-rpc-diagnostic-counts; phase7-requested-trace-envelope; phase7-stable-runtime-trace-records; all 5 production call sites exercised end-to-end

session_notes.summary: Phase B user gate confirmed ('go on'). Proceeding to TDD (Phase C). 7.5 wires the 7.3 TraceCollector into agent_loop (run/turn/provider/tool/diagnostic-linked records via an observe() helper that mirrors every runtime diagnostic as a DiagnosticLinked trace record), adds startup diagnostics before prompt output (JSON StartupDiagnostics line; RPC rpc_ready already carries them), structured SessionDiagnosticCounts in run summaries, a --trace opt-in file path (FileTraceSink) + RPC trace command (RecordingTraceSink) returning a versioned redacted envelope or a structured unsupported error, and compaction DiagnosticLinked records from the harness. All additive 0.x; SDK_SCHEMA_VERSION stays 2; trace opt-in (no behavior change when off). Working tree clean at start; baseline dirty files empty.

Phase C/D/E complete. TDD red (compile-fail: AgentLoopContext.trace absent) -> green. Wired 7.3 TraceCollector into agent_loop via AgentLoopContext.trace + Agent::set_trace_collector: run/turn/provider/tool/diagnostic-linked records, observe() mirrors every runtime diagnostic as DiagnosticLinked (lockstep, no divergence), fail-open on trace write error, fail-closed via AgentError::TraceSetup on prepare. Harness owns the collector (prepare/finish bracket per prompt, compaction DiagnosticLinked record, RecordingSink for counts). JSON: StartupDiagnostics line before prompt + SessionDiagnosticCounts in summary; RPC: rpc_ready startup_diagnostics + run_summary event with counts + trace command (versioned redacted envelope / structured unsupported_trace_request error, error_code on SdkResponse). --trace <path> CLI flag (FileTraceSink). All additive 0.x: SDK_SCHEMA_VERSION stays 2, NDJSON schema unchanged, trace OFF by default. 5-lens adversarial evaluator: 3 raw findings, 2 confirmed HIGH/MEDIUM real -> FIXED + pinned: (1) RecordingSink never reset across RPC runs (counts accumulated) -> RecordingSink::clear() at prepare_trace_run + phase7_run_summary_per_run_counts test; (2) run_summary emitted before AgentEnd drained -> drain_events before complete_run_task in run_loop join arm + shutdown_active_run, pinned by same test asserting AgentEnd precedes run_summary. 1 low finding (TraceSetup classifier arm reachability) refuted by verifier (correct exhaustiveness defense). Gates: fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, doc -D warnings clean, full workspace test 0 failures, smoke passed.

session_notes.summary: Phase B user gate confirmed ('Proceed'). Scope decision: SC1 proven at structured boundaries only (doctor NDJSON, trace DiagnosticLinked, RPC error_code, provider/runtime From->Diagnostic); 7.5 aggregate rollups (StartupDiagnostics Vec<String>, SessionDiagnosticCounts) kept as-is to respect 7.5's locked additive 0.x wire contract. 7.6 deliverables: (1) EN+ZH docs posture (local/explicit/unstable 0.x) across spec 11.3 + README + pi-alignment-matrix + opi-coding-agent README; (2) redaction FIX extending SecretRedactor (ghp_ PATs, credentialed-URL userinfo, Authorization header) + guard tests phase7_redacts_sensitive_values/phase7_doctor_redacts_sensitive_values/phase7_json_trace_redacts_sensitive_values/phase7_rpc_trace_redacts_sensitive_values; (3) shared-shape guards phase7_shared_diagnostics_used_by_{surfaces,doctor,rpc}; (4) observability_docs.rs with docs_state_local_explicit_observability + non_goals_are_not_claimed_or_implemented; (5) final fmt/clippy/doc/test/smoke. C.1a appended streaming_proxy.rs to task_owned_paths. Working tree clean at start (HEAD b32c289); baseline dirty files empty.

Phase C/D/E complete. TDD red->green per surface. Deliverables: (1) redaction FIX extending SecretRedactor (gh[pousr]_/github_pat_ PATs, credentialed-URL userinfo, authorization/proxy-authorization headers) closing the 7.4-deferred doctor package_source leak; (2) 9 phase7_* guard tests across diagnostics/doctor_cli/json_mode/rpc_jsonl/trace_envelope + new observability_docs.rs (shared-shape structural guard, docs posture positive guard EN+ZH, non-goal negative guard with Cargo.toml telemetry-crate scan + meta-guard); (3) EN+ZH observability posture (local/explicit/unstable 0.x) in spec 11.3, root README, pi-alignment-matrix Phase 7 row, coding-agent README. SC1 proven at structured boundaries only per user-approved decision (7.5 aggregate rollups kept to respect additive 0.x wire). C.1a appends: streaming_proxy.rs + docs/snapshots/phase4/opi-impl-state.json. Regression caught+fixed: spec edit broke phase4_ledger_spec_hash_matches_current_spec -> phase4 snapshot spec hash re-synced (e99b0b2d -> e525fd77), same maintenance Phase 6 performed. 6-lens adversarial evaluator: 1 confirmed MEDIUM (spec 11.3 overclaimed a non-existent 'config setting' trace trigger) -> FIXED (dropped to --trace/RPC only) + re-verified; 5 other lenses (redaction-correctness, guard-test-strength, non-goal-scope, shared-shape-decision, regression-risk) 0 confirmed. commit_type feat(workspace) (redaction src fix makes feat more accurate than init-inferred docs). Gates: fmt clean, clippy -D warnings clean, doc -D warnings clean, full workspace test 0 failures, smoke 64 passed. Not pushed.

Commits are not amended; footer is authoritative for audit. Ledger evidence.opi_evaluator now mirrors the actual footer value.
